Baruch was involved in writing Jeremiah's prophecies and reading them; he was threatened for it by the king. People who are new to their faith often feel discouraged by small difficulties that they commonly encounter at first when serving God. These complaints and fears came from his own corruption. Baruch had raised his expectations too high in this world, which made the distress and trouble he was experiencing harder to bear. The disapproval of the world would not upset us if we did not foolishly flatter ourselves with hopes of its approval and seek it out. What a folly it is then to pursue great things for ourselves here, where everything is small and nothing certain! The Lord knows the real cause of our anxiety and despair better than we do, and we should ask him to examine our hearts and restrain every wrong desire within us.
